DISCOVERY 610, RESPONDENT MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS AND INSPECTION DEMAND AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND EXPENSES . GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO SERVE COMPLETE VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $536.30 ARE TO BE PAID WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER BY EACH DEFENDANT.

DISCOVERY 610, AFTER HEARING, PLAINTIFF'S CHALLENGE PURSUANT TO CCP 170.6(A)(2) IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY AS THE DECLARATION OF COUNSEL WAS FILED ON OCTOBER 12, 2005. MR. CONNELLY AND MR. WOODS OBJECTED TO MR. CANATELLA'S ORDER. COURT WILL NOT SIGN THE ORDER IF PARTIES DO NOT HAVE A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. AT 10:08AM, ALL COUNSEL AND DENNIS BARTHOLOMEW RETURNED TO COURT. THEY DON'T HAVE A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. COURT IS CONTINUING THE EVIDENTIARY HEARING TO 08/26/05 AT 9AM. COURT IS TENTATIVELY SETTING THE TRIAL ON THE TRUST CONTEST ON 08/26/05. IF THE CASE WILL TAKE LONGER THAN ONE DAY, COURT WILL REFER THE CASE TO THE PRESIDING JUDGE FOR TRIAL DATE PER COURT

Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And For Attorney'S Fees And Expenses; Points And Auth. Declaration Of R.A. Canatella With Exhibits; Proof Of Mail Service
Off Calendar Oct-13-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard Canatella for respondent and moving party: Dennis Bartholomew
John Conneely for petitioner and opposing party: Richrd Bartholomew

HEARING HELD and Plaintiff's challenge pursuant to CCP Section 170.6(a)(2) is denied as untimely as the declaration of counsel was filed on October 12, 2005.

Respondent Dennis Bartholomew's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories as to petitioners Robert Hennum, Susan Hennum, Sharon Hennum-Yore, Chester Gutowski Jr., and Connie Gutowski: GRANTED. Complete verified responses without objection shall be served within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are waived by respondent.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Motion For Issue Sanctions For Disobeying A Court Order Compelling Further Answers To Form Interrogatories & Inspection Demands; Points And Auth; Decl. Of Richard A. Canatella & Exhibits; Req. For Judicial Notice Proof Of Service By Mail
Off Calendar Oct-13-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard Canatella for respondent and moving party: Dennis Bartholomew
John Conneely for petitioner and opposing party: Richrd Bartholomew

After Hearing, plaintiff's challenge pursuant to CCP 170.6(a)(2) is denied as untimely as the declaration of counsel was filed on October 12, 2005.

Respondent Dennis Bartholomew's motion for sanctions as to petitioner Richard Hennum is: GRANTED IN PART. The Court finds that petitioner has failed to pay the monetary sanctions in a timely fashion as Exhibit 2-A indicates that said sanctions were paid on September 30, 2005. In addition, there is no proof that responses by Richard Hennum to form interrogatories and request for production of documents have been served as previously ordered by the Court. Richard Hennum is ordered to fully comply with the terms of the August 22, 2005 order withing 15 days of notice of this order. While the Court finds that petitioner disobeyed the prior order, additional monetary sanctions will not be imposes based on the declartion of counsel. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Answer To Nonform Or Specially Prepared Interrogatories & For Atty'S Fee & Exp; P&A; Decl.
Off Calendar Sep-12-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard A. Canatella for respondent and moving party

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Respondent Dennis Bartholomew's motion to compel responses to special interrogatories as to petitioners Richard Hennum, Robert Hennum, Susan Hennum, Sharon Hennum-Yore, Chester Gutowski Jr., and Connie Gutowski is granted. No opposition has been filed. Petitioners are ordered to provide complete verified responses without objection within 21 days of notice of this order. Failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $536.30 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
